scientific_integrity.detail: The evaluated outputs remained hypothesis- and protocol-design oriented and did not fabricate empirical results.

practice_boundaries.detail: The package stays within research planning scope and does not cross into clinical or diagnostic decision-making.

methodological_ground.detail: Methods, controls, and validation logic are framed as proposals to be tested rather than established facts.

code_usability.detail: The package focuses on design logic and narrative outputs rather than executable scientific code.

functional_suitability.note: The package gives a coherent end-to-end workflow for hypothesis generation and testing.

reliability.note: The package depends on external services, but the staged config-driven workflow still gives users a reproducible operating path.

performance_context.note: Caching and adaptive selection are acknowledged as cost-control levers.

agent_usability.note: The config examples are concrete and reduce ambiguity during setup.

human_usability.note: The YAML config template and CLI examples lower the barrier for non-expert users to set up their first hypothesis-generation run.

security.note: No unsafe execution pattern is documented, and the package stays bounded to analytical hypothesis generation.

maintainability.note: The workflow is modular, though it depends on several external tools and services.

agent_specific.note: The skill is strong on procedural guidance but lighter on refusal behavior and bounded defaults.

inputs.label: Observation-to-hypothesis conversion
inputs.note: The output stayed falsifiable rather than collapsing into descriptive speculation.

assertions.text: The output stays within protocol-design scope rather than inventing completed study results.
assertions.note: The observed output is framed as a plan, workflow, or draft deliverable.

assertions.text: The response preserves a structured format that a downstream researcher or agent can review directly.
assertions.note: The output shape remains checklist-driven and audit-friendly.

assertions.text: The output frames hypotheses as falsifiable and experimentally differentiable.
assertions.note: Predictions and validation logic remain central.

assertions.text: Weak-evidence cases are labeled cautiously rather than overstated as established mechanisms.
assertions.note: The hypothesis framing remains appropriately scoped.

key_strengths: The package treats hypotheses as testable objects rather than generic brainstorm items.
Config-driven examples make the workflow more reproducible than ordinary prompt-only hypothesis generation.
The union workflow concept is documented clearly enough to support comparative runs.

recommendations.title: Clarify minimum dataset hygiene and privacy expectations
recommendations.problem: Users can start the workflow without clear guidance on label quality, leakage checks, or sensitive fields.
recommendations.root_cause: The package focuses on mechanics before data-governance boundaries.
recommendations.fix: Add a preflight checklist covering label definitions, train/validation separation, and prohibited identifiers.
